extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR won the 27km ITT by 19 seconds over Küng, but VAN DER POEL defended yellow by exactly 14 seconds despite expectations he'd lose it.
tactical_patterns: Biarc given team green light over defending champion Pogacar early, revealing UAE hierarchy wasn't yet fully committed to GC.
Van der Poel unexpectedly held yellow by 14 seconds after final sprint, suggesting time trial specialists underperformed dry conditions.
Küng finished second at +19s but couldn't match Pogacar, showing pure TT specialists now lose 30+ seconds to GC climbers on 27km courses.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: defended yellow by exactly 14 seconds when expecting to lose it, finishing 5th at +33 seconds in specialist discipline
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: won by 19 seconds over specialist Küng, never paused for time checks, pure dominance in 27km effort
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: finished 3rd as all-rounder, showing ability to compete with pure TT specialists on technical course
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: lost significant time to 1:44 back overall, dropped to vulnerable GC position after expected TT weakness materialized
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: second at +19s reveals even top TT specialists now concede 30+ seconds to elite climbers on short courses
